component_identification.content: The game includes main board, player boards, cattle decks, market cattle cards, worker tiles in three professions, hazard tiles, teepee tiles, objective cards, station master tiles, neutral and private building tiles, player discs, engines, certificate markers, and cattlemen. Setup stages multiple tile stacks and markets so options enter in balanced waves. Each player has numbered private buildings and a starting cattle deck.

core_game_loop.content: Players move cattlemen forward along the trail and resolve the action where they stop. Along the route they improve deck quality, hire workers, place buildings, remove hazards, and develop railroad position. Reaching Kansas City triggers delivery: evaluate herd value, gain money, and ship to a city for points and bonuses. Then return to start and repeat. Endgame occurs after official trigger thresholds are met.

detailed_rules.content: Movement is forward and constrained by board topology and occupied spaces. Building actions create permanent route economy and interaction points. Hiring from worker market supports strategic focus: cowboys for cattle, craftsmen for buildings, engineers for rail. Herd value depends on distinct cattle breeds in hand, so deck thinning and upgrade timing are core efficiency skills. Kansas City delivery sequence must be resolved in exact order, including certificates and train-position effects that can add penalties if rail lags behind delivery ambition. Hazard and teepee spaces change route costs and can be converted into points or value by effects. Objective cards reward structured planning but can be traps if they conflict with your movement and deck tempo. Common mistakes are backward planning too late for hand quality, underdeveloped train position, and inefficient private-building placement off your natural path.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Final scoring combines delivery network results, objectives, board and tile bonuses, railroad progression, and other listed endgame sources. Endgame trigger depends on depletion and player-count settings in official setup. Highest victory points wins using standard tie-break steps.

beginner_strategy.content: Upgrade cattle early so every delivery improves. Keep train progress close to delivery targets to avoid repeated penalties. Specialize workers but stay flexible with market opportunities. Place private buildings where your route already goes. Use certificates deliberately for high-value delivery turns.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
